description.task: Implement a BinarySearchTree class supporting insert, search, and inorder traversal. Verify that inorder traversal of a BST always produces sorted output.
instructions: search(val): start at root; if null return false; if val==node.val return true; if val<node.val recurse left; else recurse right
inorderHelper: if node==null return; recurse left; print node.val+' '; recurse right
insert is already implemented — do not modify
Inorder of BST with [5,3,7,1,4,6,8] produces sorted: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 — wait: 1 3 4 5 6 7 8
description.hint: search: Node curr=root; while(curr!=null){if(val==curr.val)return true; curr=val<curr.val?curr.left:curr.right;} return false;
description.expectedOutput:
Sorted inorder: 1 3 4 5 6 7 8 
Search 4: true
Search 9: false

description.task: Implement BST delete operation handling three cases: (1) leaf node, (2) node with one child, (3) node with two children (replace with inorder successor — smallest value in right subtree).
instructions: Case 3: int successor = findMin(root.right); root.val = successor; root.right = delete(root.right, successor);
Cases 1 and 2 are already handled above the TODO
Deleting 3 (has children 1 and 4): successor is 4 (min of right subtree {4}), replace 3 with 4, result: 1 4 5 6 7 8
Deleting 5 (root, has children): successor is 6, result: 1 4 6 7 8
description.expectedOutput:
Before delete 3: 1 3 4 5 6 7 8 
After delete 3: 1 4 5 6 7 8 
After delete 5: 1 4 6 7 8

description.task: Implement a RankBST class that: counts rank (how many elements are <= value), finds floor (greatest element <= target), and ceil (smallest element >= target) in a BST.
instructions: floor(root, target): if root==null return -1; if root.val==target return root.val; if root.val>target recurse left; else candidate=root.val, recurse right for better; return Math.max(candidate, floor(right,target))
ceil(root, target): symmetric — if root.val==target return root.val; if root.val<target recurse right; else candidate=root.val, recurse left for smaller; take Math.min
description.hint: floor: if(root==null)return -1; if(root.val==target)return root.val; if(root.val>target)return floor(root.left,target); int right=floor(root.right,target); return right==-1?root.val:right;
description.expectedOutput:
Rank of 4: 3
Rank of 7: 6
Floor of 8: 7
Ceil of 8: 9
Floor of 1: 1
Ceil of 10: -1
